arguments.task: Visit the official Brex developer documentation at https://developer.brex.com and gather evidence for: API authentication methods, whether the API is REST or GraphQL, available API breadth/endpoints/products, rate limits, and any getting started requirements. Then visit official Brex pricing, support, GitHub, or MCP-related official pages if linked or discoverable from official domains to verify access gating/self-serve requirements and whether an official MCP server exists. Return exact page URLs and concise quoted findings.

Return concise bullet findings with the exact page URL for each.

raw_result.description: Corporate spend management platform with APIs for cards, transactions, payments, travel, budgets, onboarding, and expense workflows.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs show developer authentication via a Brex user token used as a Bearer token, generated from the Brex dashboard after accepting the API Agreement. Brex also documents separate partner authentication, but the exact public auth flow details were not fully verified from gathered evidence.
raw_result.gating_reason: Brex pricing indicates an Essentials plan at $0/user, and developer docs describe self-serve token setup from the Brex dashboard under Developer > Settings for account admins/card admins.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API docs cover multiple product areas including Accounting, Budgets & Limits, Expenses, Fields, Onboarding, Payments, Team, Transactions, Travel, and Webhooks.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official Brex MCP documentation exists and points to an MCP server at api.brex.com/mcp, with setup guides for Claude Code, Claude Desktop, ChatGPT, Cursor, and Codex; docs note enabling a beta feature in Brex settings.

raw_result.blocker: A first toolkit appears buildable from public docs, but setup requires a Brex account with appropriate admin/card-admin permissions and token generation in-product; partner auth details remain partially unverified from current evidence.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Developer docs reportedly state limits of 1,000 requests per 60 seconds, 1,000 transfers per 24 hours, 100 international wires per 24 hours, and 5,000 cards per 24 hours.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official developer portal is comprehensive and includes quickstart, auth, rate limits, support, reference docs, and an official MCP page. Support docs reportedly list developer-support@brex.com and a Slack community. Public GitHub presence appears minimal compared with the main developer portal.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Some details were inferred from browser-captured evidence rather than directly quoted in-chat, especially the exact partner authentication flow and the pricing/API-access relationship. Human verification is recommended before treating self-serve access and auth breadth as fully settled.
